title: "make test" fails on cmake_protect_lib starting with 93d50c449
body:
Under stock Debian (pre-)Bookworm,
`git clean -fdx && autoconf && ./configure --prefix=$HOME/test && make -j 6 && make test`
output ends with
```
make[1]: Entering directory '/redacted/verilator/examples/cmake_protect_lib'

-- Verilator CMake protect_lib example
[...]
CMake Error at /redacted/verilator/verilator-config.cmake:244 (message):
  Verilator command failed (return code=Segmentation fault)
Call Stack (most recent call first):
  CMakeLists.txt:58 (verilate)
```
starting at commit 93d50c449.
That's a big commit, and I have no idea how it triggers the failure.
The error message steers me to a CMakeError.log file, and the fault looks like
```
Building CXX object CMakeFiles/cmTC_85bd6.dir/src.cxx.o
/usr/bin/c++ -D_mt   -o CMakeFiles/cmTC_85bd6.dir/src.cxx.o -c /redacted/verilator/examples/cmake_protect_lib/build/CMakeFiles/CMakeScratch/TryCompile-buqlpY/src.cxx
Linking CXX executable cmTC_85bd6
/usr/bin/cmake -E cmake_link_script CMakeFiles/cmTC_85bd6.dir/link.txt --verbose=1
/usr/bin/c++ CMakeFiles/cmTC_85bd6.dir/src.cxx.o -o cmTC_85bd6  -mt 
c++: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-mt’; did you mean ‘-MT’?
gmake[3]: *** [CMakeFiles/cmTC_85bd6.dir/build.make:99: cmTC_85bd6] Error 1
gmake[3]: Leaving directory '/redacted/verilator/examples/cmake_protect_lib/build/CMakeFiles/CMakeScratch/TryCompile-buqlpY'
gmake[2]: *** [Makefile:127: cmTC_85bd6/fast] Error 2
gmake[2]: Leaving directory '/redacted/verilator/examples/cmake_protect_lib/build/CMakeFiles/CMakeScratch/TryCompile-buqlpY'
```
I hope this helps.  LMK if more info is needed.
Oh, c++ in this case is `c++ (Debian 12.2.0-14) 12.2.0`
